commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Telegram-local block-streaming resolver and updates Telegram dispatch, doctor warnings, and tests so explicit Telegram partial/progress streaming can override the legacy global block-streaming default.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +81. Total +99 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main applies the global block default before Telegram draft eligibility, so an explicit partial Telegram config can still be blocked. The PR-head regression is also source-clear because an unset Telegram config resolves to the default `partial` mode and disables the global default.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human PR follow-up because the branch has a compatibility bug and the contributor must add real Telegram proof; automation cannot supply that contributor-environment proof.

target.title: fix(telegram): keep partial streams live when block defaults are on
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users who configured partial streaming could still see blocked delivery when a legacy global block-streaming default was enabled.

## Why This Change Was Made

Telegram now resolves block streaming through a Telegram-owned helper that lets explicit Telegram block-streaming config win, lets `streaming.mode: "partial"` or `"progress"` disable legacy block delivery, and keeps doctor warnings aligned with runtime behavior.

## User Impact

Telegram partial streaming now behaves as one live updating preview unless block streaming is explicitly enabled for Telegram. Existing explicit block-streaming setups continue to work.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts` passed: 168 tests.
- `git diff HEAD --check` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode uncommitted --engine copilot` passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
